- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Phase 4a (L3, §6c-4) — independence-collapse by content near-duplication.
2	
3	Credibility-weighted sourcing redesign, plan
4	``docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md`` Phase 4a.
5	
6	WHAT THIS DOES
7	==============
8	Given a list of generator-visible evidence rows, cluster the near-DUPLICATE
9	content copies (syndication / press-release rehash / verbatim republication)
10	into ``origin clusters`` so a later weighted tally (L5, Phase 6) counts each
11	independent ORIGIN exactly once and cannot be inflated by copies.
12	
13	The collapse is layered on the existing host-collapse primitive
14	(``authority.corroboration``: ``count_independent_hosts`` /
15	``registrable_domain``). Two rows join the same origin cluster when EITHER
16	
17	  1. they share a registrable domain (eTLD+1 host-collapse — the existing
18	     primitive; subdomains of one institution are the same origin), OR
19	  2. their text content is a near-duplicate: TF-IDF cosine >= the configured
20	     similarity threshold (default ``DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD`` = 0.85),
21	
22	EXCEPT a curated acceptable-mirror allowlist (arXiv / SSRN / PMC by default)
23	is NOT collapsed on content alone across DIFFERENT registrable domains — a
24	legitimate mirror of the same paper on two scholarly hosts stays two
25	independent origins rather than being falsely flagged a copy (the
26	echo-collapse false-positive bound, plan §6 RISKS).
27	
28	CANONICAL-ORIGIN INVARIANT (the load-bearing safety property)
29	=============================================================
30	Each cluster designates exactly ONE **canonical origin**. When ANY member
31	carries a parseable publication date, the canonical is the EARLIEST-dated
32	member and ``authority_score`` is NOT consulted — a DATED cluster keeps
33	STRICT copy-invariance (adding a same/later/undated copy of ANY authority
34	leaves the canonical + cluster_mass unchanged). When EVERY member is undated
35	there is no date to identify the seed, so the canonical is the LOWEST-
36	``authority_score`` member (conservative-min, Codex #1161): a higher-authority
37	copy can NEVER become canonical or inflate cluster_mass, and the worst a copy
38	can do is LOWER the mass (monotonic non-increase) — never inflate.
39	
40	Therefore: adding a copied row to an existing cluster — **even a copy whose
41	own ``authority_score`` is HIGHER than the cluster's canonical origin** —
42	does NOT change the cluster set nor its canonical origin nor inflate its
43	mass. A high-authority verbatim republisher is still derivative; only its own
44	*independent* content
45	would form a new cluster. This is exactly what lets the L5 weighted tally
46	(``cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin)``, copies contribute
47	zero) be uninflatable by copies. The invariant is proven by
48	``test_independence_collapse.py``.
49	
50	OUTPUT (per the Phase-4a emitted contract)
51	==========================================
52	``collapse_independent_origins`` returns an :class:`IndependenceCollapseResult`
53	carrying, for every input row (by index):
54	  - a stable ``origin_cluster_id`` (deterministic, derived from the canonical
55	    member so it is stable under copy-additions),
56	  - the canonical-origin row index for that cluster,
57	  - an ``is_canonical_origin`` / ``is_derivative_copy`` flag,
58	and an ``independent_origin_count`` = number of distinct origin clusters.
59	
60	PURITY / SAFETY
61	===============
62	Pure: constructs no client, opens no network, loads no model, calls no LLM,
63	mutates none of the caller's rows (membership/flags are emitted in a fresh
64	structure). snake_case, explicit imports, no host/TLD literals in code (the
65	PSL ``gov_suffixes`` and the mirror allowlist are passed in by the caller).
66	
67	DEFAULT-OFF: this module is inert library code. Nothing in the production
68	pipeline imports or invokes it; it acts ONLY when a flagged caller calls
69	``collapse_independent_origins``. It touches NO faithfulness gate
70	(strict_verify / 4-role / two-family / corpus_approval are untouched) and
71	NEVER drops a source — it only annotates duplicated corroboration.
72	"""
73	from __future__ import annotations
74	
75	import datetime
76	import math
77	import re
78	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
79	from typing import Any
80	from urllib.parse import urlparse
81	
82	from src.polaris_graph.authority.corroboration import registrable_domain
83	
84	# ── Module constants (LAW VI — named, not magic numbers) ────────────────────
85	# Cosine-similarity threshold at/above which two texts are a content copy.
86	DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD: float = 0.85
87	# Curated acceptable-mirror domains: legitimate cross-host mirrors of the same
88	# scholarly artefact that must NOT be over-collapsed on content alone (plan §6
89	# echo-collapse false-positive bound). Passed-in overrides win; this is only
90	# the documented default.
91	#
92	# IMPORTANT: these are compared against each row's REGISTRABLE DOMAIN (the
93	# eTLD+1 produced by ``corroboration.registrable_domain``), so they MUST be
94	# written in eTLD+1 form. PMC lives at ``ncbi.nlm.nih.gov``, whose eTLD+1
95	# (without a multi-level PSL suffix match) collapses to ``nih.gov`` — so the
96	# allowlist entry for PMC is ``nih.gov`` (the registrable-domain key actually
97	# seen at comparison time), NOT the full ``ncbi.nlm.nih.gov`` host. Writing the
98	# full host here would silently never match and re-open the false-positive.
99	DEFAULT_ACCEPTABLE_MIRROR_DOMAINS: tuple[str, ...] = (
100	    "arxiv.org",   # arXiv preprint mirror
101	    "ssrn.com",    # SSRN working-paper mirror
102	    "nih.gov",     # PMC (ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) eTLD+1 -> nih.gov
103	)
104	# Row keys consulted for the row's text, in priority order. First non-empty
105	# wins. Mirrors finding_dedup's row-shape expectations. ``direct_quote`` (the
106	# CLAIM-LOCAL span when the caller has set it) is FIRST, so the cosine compares
107	# claim-local span text over chrome-laden full-row text where available
108	# (I-deepfix-001 B9(b)).
109	_TEXT_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("direct_quote", "statement", "text", "snippet")
110	# I-deepfix-001 B9(b): row keys consulted for a DOI identity, in priority order.
111	# Two rows with DISTINCT non-empty DOIs are DIFFERENT works and must NEVER be
112	# collapsed into one origin cluster on body-cosine alone (the origin_cluster_id
113	# collision: a near-dup body of mostly chrome — masthead/affiliation/DOI lines —
114	# crossing the 0.85 cosine and folding 4 distinct DOIs under one canonical).
115	_DOI_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("doi", "DOI")
116	# Row keys consulted for an explicit publication-order signal, in priority
117	# order. First parseable value wins; absence falls back to corpus order.
118	_ORDER_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = (
119	    "published_date",
120	    "publication_date",
121	    "published",
122	    "date",
123	)
124	# A token is a maximal run of word characters; lowercased. Pure-stdlib
125	# tokeniser — no model, no network.
126	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_]+", re.UNICODE)
127	
128	
129	@dataclass
130	class OriginCluster:
131	    """One cluster of rows that share a single independent origin."""
132	
133	    origin_cluster_id: str
134	    canonical_index: int            # input row index of the canonical origin
135	    member_indices: list[int]       # all input row indices in the cluster
136	    copy_indices: list[int]         # derivative-copy members (canonical excluded)
137	    member_hosts: list[str]         # sorted unique registrable-domains present
138	
139	
140	@dataclass
141	class RowOriginAssignment:
142	    """Per-row origin assignment (emitted contract — one per input row)."""
143	
144	    row_index: int
145	    origin_cluster_id: str
146	    canonical_index: int
147	    is_canonical_origin: bool
148	    is_derivative_copy: bool
149	
150	
151	@dataclass
152	class IndependenceCollapseResult:
153	    """Result of :func:`collapse_independent_origins`."""
154	
155	    clusters: list[OriginCluster]
156	    assignments: list[RowOriginAssignment]  # ordered by input row index
157	    raw_row_count: int
158	    independent_origin_count: int            # == number of distinct clusters
159	    similarity_threshold: float = field(default=DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD)
160	
161	
162	# ── Pure helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	
165	def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
166	    """Bare hostname (lowercased, ``www.`` stripped) from a URL; ``""`` if none.
167	
168	    ``registrable_domain`` expects a HOST, not a full URL, so this reduction
169	    must run first (mirrors ``finding_dedup._host_of``).
170	    """
171	    if not url:
172	        return ""
173	    host = (urlparse(url).hostname or "").lower().strip()
174	    if host.startswith("www."):
175	        host = host[4:]
176	    return host
177	
178	
179	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
180	    """First non-empty text field of a row (priority order ``_TEXT_KEYS``)."""
181	    for key in _TEXT_KEYS:
182	        value = row.get(key)
183	        if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
184	            return value
185	    return ""
186	
187	
188	def _row_domain(row: dict[str, Any], gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:
189	    """Registrable domain (eTLD+1) for a row's ``source_url``; ``""`` if none."""
190	    return registrable_domain(_host_of(str(row.get("source_url", ""))), gov_suffixes)
191	
192	
193	def _row_doi(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
194	    """I-deepfix-001 B9(b): normalized DOI identity of a row (lower-cased, ``doi:`` / URL prefixes
195	    stripped), or ``""`` when the row carries no DOI. Two rows with DISTINCT non-empty DOIs are
196	    different works and must never join one origin cluster on body-cosine alone."""
197	    for key in _DOI_KEYS:
198	        value = row.get(key)
199	        if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
200	            doi = value.strip().lower()
201	            # Strip common prefixes so "10.1/x", "doi:10.1/x", and "https://doi.org/10.1/x" compare equal.
202	            for prefix in ("https://doi.org/", "http://doi.org/", "https://dx.doi.org/", "doi.org/", "doi:"):
203	                if doi.startswith(prefix):
204	                    doi = doi[len(prefix):]
205	                    break
206	            return doi.strip("/").strip()
207	    return ""
208	
209	
210	def _parse_order_date(value: Any) -> tuple[int, int, int] | None:
211	    """Parse a publication-order value to a sortable ``(year, month, day)``, or ``None``
212	    when it is not CONFIDENTLY parseable (then the row is treated as undated and sorts
213	    last). Only UNAMBIGUOUS year-first formats are accepted — ISO ``YYYY-MM-DD`` /
214	    ``YYYY/MM/DD`` / ``YYYY-MM`` / ``YYYY``, or an int/float year. Ambiguous or malformed
215	    values (e.g. ``01/01/2099`` day-first, or free text) are REJECTED so they cannot
216	    lexically outrank a real ISO date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-2 P1).
217	    """
218	    if isinstance(value, bool):
219	        return None
220	    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
221	        year = int(value)
222	        return (year, 1, 1) if 1000 <= year <= 9999 else None
223	    if not isinstance(value, str):
224	        return None
225	    match = re.match(r"^(\d{4})(?:[-/](\d{1,2})(?:[-/](\d{1,2}))?)?$", value.strip())
226	    if not match:
227	        return None
228	    year = int(match.group(1))
229	    month = int(match.group(2) or 1)
230	    day = int(match.group(3) or 1)
231	    try:
232	        # REAL calendar validation — an ISO-shaped but non-calendar date (e.g. 2024-02-31,
233	        # Feb has no 31st; or 2024-13-01) raises and is treated as undated, so it cannot
234	        # outrank a genuine date and steal the canonical origin (Codex iter-3 P1).
235	        datetime.date(year, month, day)
236	    except ValueError:
237	        return None
238	    return (year, month, day)
239	
240	
241	def _order_key(row: dict[str, Any], row_index: int) -> tuple:
242	    """Deterministic canonical-selection order key (canonical = ``min`` over members).
243	
244	    A DATED row sorts before any undated row; among dated rows the earliest CALENDAR date
245	    wins and ``authority_score`` is NEVER consulted (so a higher-authority but later-dated
246	    copy can never steal the canonical origin). Same-date ties break on the STABLE
247	    evidence_id, then the input index. Codex iter-1/2/3 fixes: dated-beats-undated;
248	    malformed/non-ISO and non-calendar dates are treated as undated.
249	
250	    When EVERY member of a cluster is undated there is no date to identify the seed
251	    positionally, so the canonical is the LOWEST-``authority_score`` member: a higher-
252	    authority copy can then never become the canonical origin and inflate ``cluster_mass``
253	    (the load-bearing copy-invariance invariant — Codex iter-4 P1). The evidence_id breaks
254	    authority ties (NOT the input index), so a prepended copy cannot win on position. A
255	    later-added LOWER-authority copy may relabel the cluster to itself, but that only ever
256	    LOWERS the mass — monotonically non-increasing under additions, never inflatable.
257	
258	    Returns a 5-tuple ``(has_no_date, (y, m, d), auth_rank, evidence_id, row_index)``; the
259	    ``auth_rank`` slot is a constant ``0.0`` for DATED rows (date decides) and the real
260	    authority for undated rows (lowest wins).
261	    """
262	    eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
263	    for key in _ORDER_KEYS:
264	        parsed = _parse_order_date(row.get(key))
265	        if parsed is not None:
266	            return (0, parsed, 0.0, eid, row_index)
267	    authority = row.get("authority_score")
268	    auth_rank = float(authority) if isinstance(authority, (int, float)) else 0.0
269	    return (1, (9999, 12, 31), auth_rank, eid, row_index)
270	
271	
272	def _tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
273	    """Lowercased word-character tokens. Pure stdlib; deterministic."""
274	    return [t.lower() for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(text)]
275	
276	
277	def _term_frequencies(tokens: list[str]) -> dict[str, int]:
278	    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
279	    for tok in tokens:
280	        counts[tok] = counts.get(tok, 0) + 1
281	    return counts
282	
283	
284	def _tfidf_cosine_matrix(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
285	    """Pairwise TF-IDF cosine similarity over ``texts`` (pure Python).
286	
287	    TF = raw term count per document. IDF = ``ln((1 + N) / (1 + df)) + 1``
288	    (the smoothed, always-positive scikit-learn convention) so a single-doc
289	    corpus still yields well-defined, non-degenerate vectors. Vectors are
290	    L2-normalised; cosine of two normalised vectors is their dot product.
291	    An empty document has a zero vector and cosine 0 with everything.
292	    """
293	    n = len(texts)
294	    token_lists = [_tokenize(t) for t in texts]
295	    # Document frequency per term.
296	    doc_freq: dict[str, int] = {}
297	    for tokens in token_lists:
298	        for term in set(tokens):
299	            doc_freq[term] = doc_freq.get(term, 0) + 1
300	    idf: dict[str, float] = {
301	        term: math.log((1 + n) / (1 + df)) + 1.0 for term, df in doc_freq.items()
302	    }
303	    # Build L2-normalised TF-IDF vectors as sparse dicts.
304	    vectors: list[dict[str, float]] = []
305	    for tokens in token_lists:
306	        tf = _term_frequencies(tokens)
307	        vec = {term: count * idf[term] for term, count in tf.items()}
308	        norm = math.sqrt(sum(w * w for w in vec.values()))
309	        if norm > 0.0:
310	            vec = {term: w / norm for term, w in vec.items()}
311	        vectors.append(vec)
312	    # Pairwise cosine = dot product of normalised vectors.
313	    matrix = [[0.0] * n for _ in range(n)]
314	    for i in range(n):
315	        matrix[i][i] = 1.0 if vectors[i] else 0.0
316	        for j in range(i + 1, n):
317	            a, b = vectors[i], vectors[j]
318	            # Iterate the smaller dict for the dot product.
319	            if len(a) > len(b):
320	                a, b = b, a
321	            dot = sum(weight * b.get(term, 0.0) for term, weight in a.items())
322	            sim = 0.0 if dot < 0.0 else (1.0 if dot > 1.0 else dot)
323	            matrix[i][j] = sim
324	            matrix[j][i] = sim
325	    return matrix
326	
327	
328	class _UnionFind:
329	    """Minimal disjoint-set for transitive content-copy clustering."""
330	
331	    def __init__(self, n: int) -> None:
332	        self._parent = list(range(n))
333	
334	    def find(self, x: int) -> int:
335	        root = x
336	        while self._parent[root] != root:
337	            root = self._parent[root]
338	        # Path compression.
339	        while self._parent[x] != root:
340	            self._parent[x], x = root, self._parent[x]
341	        return root
342	
343	    def union(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
344	        ra, rb = self.find(a), self.find(b)
345	        if ra != rb:
346	            # Attach the higher-index root under the lower so the canonical
347	            # (lowest order-key) tends to stay near the root; canonical choice
348	            # below is explicit regardless, this only keeps roots stable.
349	            lo, hi = (ra, rb) if ra < rb else (rb, ra)
350	            self._parent[hi] = lo
351	
352	
353	# ── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
354	
355	
356	def collapse_independent_origins(
357	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
358	    *,
359	    gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...],
360	    similarity_threshold: float = DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD,
361	    acceptable_mirror_domains: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_ACCEPTABLE_MIRROR_DOMAINS,
362	) -> IndependenceCollapseResult:
363	    """Cluster rows into independent origins; flag derivative copies.
364	
365	    Args:
366	        rows: generator-visible evidence rows. Each may carry ``evidence_id``,
367	            ``source_url``, text (``direct_quote``/``statement``/``text``/
368	            ``snippet``), an optional publication-order key
369	            (``published_date``/``publication_date``/``published``/``date``),
370	            and an optional ``authority_score`` (NOT used for clustering or
371	            canonical choice — only carried through by the caller).
372	        gov_suffixes: PSL multi-level gov-suffix tuple from
373	            ``authority.data_loader.load_authority_data()["psl_gov_suffixes"]``
374	            — passed in so this module hardcodes NO host/TLD literals.
375	        similarity_threshold: TF-IDF cosine at/above which two texts are a
376	            content copy (LAW VI — caller-configurable; default 0.85).
377	        acceptable_mirror_domains: registrable domains whose cross-host
378	            content near-duplicates are legitimate mirrors and are NOT
379	            collapsed on content alone (false-positive bound).
380	
381	    Returns:
382	        :class:`IndependenceCollapseResult` with one
383	        :class:`RowOriginAssignment` per input row (ordered by row index),
384	        the :class:`OriginCluster` list, and the ``independent_origin_count``.
385	    """
386	    rows = list(rows or [])
387	    n = len(rows)
388	    threshold = float(similarity_threshold)
389	    mirror_set = {d.lower() for d in acceptable_mirror_domains}
390	
391	    if n == 0:
392	        return IndependenceCollapseResult(
393	            clusters=[],
394	            assignments=[],
395	            raw_row_count=0,
396	            independent_origin_count=0,
397	            similarity_threshold=threshold,
398	        )
399	
400	    domains = [_row_domain(rows[i], gov_suffixes) for i in range(n)]
401	    texts = [_row_text(rows[i]) for i in range(n)]
402	    dois = [_row_doi(rows[i]) for i in range(n)]  # I-deepfix-001 B9(b): per-row DOI identity
403	    sim = _tfidf_cosine_matrix(texts)
404	
405	    uf = _UnionFind(n)
406	    for i in range(n):
407	        for j in range(i + 1, n):
408	            # I-deepfix-001 B9(b): HARD GUARD against the origin_cluster_id collision — two rows with
409	            # DISTINCT non-empty DOIs are DIFFERENT works and can NEVER share one origin cluster, on
410	            # ANY signal (host-collapse OR body-cosine). This prevents a near-dup body of mostly chrome
411	            # (masthead / affiliation / DOI lines crossing the 0.85 cosine) from folding 4 distinct DOIs
412	            # under one canonical (the observed origin::ev_009-reused-across-4-works defect). A blank DOI
413	            # on either side is NOT a distinct-DOI signal, so the existing host / cosine behaviour is
414	            # byte-identical for rows that carry no DOI (the common case).
415	            distinct_dois = bool(dois[i]) and bool(dois[j]) and dois[i] != dois[j]
416	            if distinct_dois:
417	                continue
418	            # I-deepfix-001 B9(b)/(c): SAME non-empty DOI is a POSITIVE union trigger that OVERRIDES the
419	            # scholarly-mirror allowlist skip below — two rows carrying the identical DOI are literally
420	            # the SAME artifact (an arXiv preprint + its syndication / PMC mirror of one paper), so they
421	            # MUST share one origin cluster even across distinct registrable domains. This is what makes
422	            # the B9(c) mirror-cite collapse actually FIRE on the canonical cross-mirror same-DOI pair
423	            # (the "[11][12]" illusion). STRENGTHENS the no-inflation invariant — collapsing a true copy
424	            # never inflates mass (copies contribute zero). A blank DOI on either side is inert (the
425	            # cosine / host behaviour below is byte-identical for the no-DOI common case).
426	            same_doi = bool(dois[i]) and dois[i] == dois[j]
427	            if same_doi:
428	                uf.union(i, j)
429	                continue
430	            same_domain = bool(domains[i]) and domains[i] == domains[j]
431	            if same_domain:
432	                # Same registrable origin (host-collapse primitive): one origin.
433	                uf.union(i, j)
434	                continue
435	            # Cross-domain: collapse only on content near-duplication. A curated
436	            # acceptable-mirror host (arXiv/SSRN/PMC) only ever collapses with its
437	            # OWN registrable domain (the same_domain branch above), NEVER via
438	            # cross-domain content. Otherwise a non-mirror blog that copies the
439	            # content of TWO different mirror hosts would TRANSITIVELY bridge them
440	            # into one union-find cluster (Codex iter-1 P2). Skipping any
441	            # cross-domain union that touches a mirror keeps legitimate mirrors
442	            # independent on content alone; the rare non-mirror copy of mirror
443	            # content simply stays its own origin — a safe under-collapse, never a
444	            # mirror false-merge.
445	            either_mirror = domains[i] in mirror_set or domains[j] in mirror_set
446	            if either_mirror:
447	                continue
448	            if texts[i] and texts[j] and sim[i][j] >= threshold:
449	                uf.union(i, j)
450	
451	    # Group members by union-find root.
452	    members_by_root: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
453	    for i in range(n):
454	        members_by_root.setdefault(uf.find(i), []).append(i)
455	
456	    clusters: list[OriginCluster] = []
457	    canonical_of: dict[int, int] = {}      # member index -> canonical index
458	    cluster_id_of: dict[int, str] = {}     # member index -> origin_cluster_id
459	
460	    for member_indices in members_by_root.values():
461	        member_indices = sorted(member_indices)
462	        # Canonical = earliest-dated origin (authority not consulted), or for an all-undated
463	        # cluster the LOWEST-authority member (conservative-min, no inflation — Codex #1161).
464	        canonical_index = min(
465	            member_indices, key=lambda idx: _order_key(rows[idx], idx)
466	        )
467	        # Stable, copy-immune id: derived from the canonical row's EVIDENCE IDENTITY, NOT its
468	        # input position — so a copy added BEFORE the canonical (prepended) does not shift the
469	        # index and change the id (Codex iter-2 P2). A missing evidence_id is a FAIL-LOUD data
470	        # error (Codex #1161), never a position-relative fallback.
471	        canonical_eid = str(rows[canonical_index].get("evidence_id", "") or "").strip()
472	        if not canonical_eid:
473	            raise ValueError(
474	                "independence_collapse: canonical row is missing 'evidence_id'; a stable "
475	                "origin_cluster_id requires every evidence row to carry evidence_id "
476	                "(Codex #1161 — a positional fallback id is not copy-stable)."
477	            )
478	        origin_cluster_id = f"origin::{canonical_eid}"
479	        copy_indices = [i for i in member_indices if i != canonical_index]
480	        member_hosts = sorted({domains[i] for i in member_indices} - {""})
481	        clusters.append(
482	            OriginCluster(
483	                origin_cluster_id=origin_cluster_id,
484	                canonical_index=canonical_index,
485	                member_indices=member_indices,
486	                copy_indices=copy_indices,
487	                member_hosts=member_hosts,
488	            )
489	        )
490	        for i in member_indices:
491	            canonical_of[i] = canonical_index
492	            cluster_id_of[i] = origin_cluster_id
493	
494	    clusters.sort(key=lambda c: c.canonical_index)
495	
496	    assignments = [
497	        RowOriginAssignment(
498	            row_index=i,
499	            origin_cluster_id=cluster_id_of[i],
500	            canonical_index=canonical_of[i],
501	            is_canonical_origin=(canonical_of[i] == i),
502	            is_derivative_copy=(canonical_of[i] != i),
503	        )
504	        for i in range(n)
505	    ]
506	
507	    return IndependenceCollapseResult(
508	        clusters=clusters,
509	        assignments=assignments,
510	        raw_row_count=n,
511	        independent_origin_count=len(clusters),
512	        similarity_threshold=threshold,
513	    )

2	
3	A data-driven, LAW-VI POSITIVE institutional-authority weight signal that is ORTHOGONAL to the
4	T1-T7 clinical-primary tier. The tier classifier is a hardcoded deny-list demoter: credible
5	NON-journal institutions (WEF/OECD/ILO/IMF/World Bank/UN/WHO, national statistical agencies,
6	major think-tanks, reputable news mastheads) are absent from every tier set, so they land at
7	UNKNOWN (0.20) — the SAME low band as an anonymous blog. Because ``weight_mass.cluster_mass =
8	authority_score`` and the selector fills slots by weight, a mis-low WEIGHT is a de-facto SOFT
9	FILTER: credible institutions sink and their facets never reach composition.

192	def _clamp01(value: object) -> float | None:
193	    """Coerce to a float in [0, 1]; None/garbage/out-of-range -> None (fail-soft: a malformed
194	    override entry is simply ignored, never a wrong-weight)."""
195	    try:
196	        x = float(value)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
197	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
198	        return None
199	    if x != x:  # NaN
200	        return None
201	    return 0.0 if x < 0.0 else (1.0 if x > 1.0 else x)

204	def _band_values() -> dict[str, float]:
205	    """The band_key -> weight map (defaults merged with the ``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_BANDS``
206	    JSON override; a malformed override entry is ignored, never zeroing a band)."""
207	    bands = dict(_DEFAULT_BANDS)
208	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_BANDS, "").strip()
209	    if raw:
210	        try:
211	            override = json.loads(raw)
212	            if isinstance(override, dict):
213	                for key, val in override.items():
214	                    clamped = _clamp01(val)
215	                    if clamped is not None:
216	                        bands[str(key).strip().lower()] = clamped
217	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
218	            pass  # malformed JSON -> defaults (fail-safe: never an empty/zeroed band map)
219	    return bands

225	    string OR a raw float (stored under a synthetic band key so it flows through ``_band_values``)."""

231	            if isinstance(override, dict):
232	                for host, band in override.items():
233	                    registry[str(host).strip().lower().lstrip(".")] = str(band).strip().lower()
234	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
235	            pass  # malformed JSON -> defaults only (fail-safe)
236	    return registry

239	def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
240	    """The lowercased hostname of ``url`` (empty on a blank/malformed URL)."""
241	    if not url:
242	        return ""
243	    try:
244	        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}")
245	        return (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
246	    except ValueError:
247	        return ""

250	def _suffix_match(host: str, registry: dict[str, str]) -> str | None:
251	    """Return the band key for ``host`` (or any parent domain) in ``registry``; else None.
252	    Parent-domain aware: ``reports.weforum.org`` matches the ``weforum.org`` entry."""
253	    if not host:
254	        return None
255	    if host in registry:
256	        return registry[host]
257	    parts = host.split(".")
258	    for i in range(1, len(parts)):
259	        parent = ".".join(parts[i:])
260	        if parent in registry:
261	            return registry[parent]
262	    return None

265	def _rule_band(host: str) -> str | None:
266	    """I-deepfix-003: the additive suffix-rule band for ``host`` (``*.gov`` / ``ed.gov`` ->
267	    ``government``; ``*.edu`` -> ``university``), else None. RAISE-ONLY like every band; applied
268	    only AFTER the enumerated registry (an explicit entry always wins). Pure — no kill-switch."""
269	    if not host:
270	        return None
271	    if host == "ed.gov" or host.endswith(_RULE_GOV_SUFFIX):
272	        return "government"
273	    if host.endswith(_RULE_EDU_SUFFIX):
274	        return "university"
275	    return None

278	def _resolve_band(url: str) -> str | None:
279	    """The institutional band KEY for ``url`` — the enumerated registry FIRST (an explicit entry
280	    always wins), then the additive ``*.gov`` / ``*.edu`` suffix rule. None when the host is not a
281	    recognised institution. Pure classification: NOT gated by any kill-switch (each caller applies
282	    its own switch — the WEIGHT leg gates ``institutional_authority_for_url`` on
283	    ``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_WEIGHT``; the TIER-floor leg in ``credibility_pass`` gates on
284	    ``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_TIER``)."""
285	    host = _host_of(url)
286	    if not host:
287	        return None
288	    band_key = _suffix_match(host, _registry())
289	    if band_key is not None:
290	        return band_key
291	    return _rule_band(host)

294	def institutional_band_for_url(url: str) -> str | None:
295	    """I-deepfix-003 (#1374): the institutional band KEY (``igo`` / ``statistical_agency`` /
296	    ``government`` / ``think_tank`` / ``news_masthead`` / ``university``) for ``url``'s host, or None
297	    when the host is not a recognised institution.
298	
299	    This surfaces the band NAME (the WEIGHT accessor ``institutional_authority_for_url`` returns only
300	    the numeric weight). The credibility-pass TIER-floor leg maps this key to a RAISE-ONLY anchor-
301	    eligible tier (igo/statistical_agency/government -> T3; think_tank/news_masthead/university -> T3)
302	    and to the explicit ``authority_note`` label. An env-override registry entry that stores a raw
303	    float (not a named band) is returned verbatim; the TIER map has no entry for it, so it drives the
304	    WEIGHT leg only (no tier floor). Pure — the caller applies the kill-switch."""
305	    return _resolve_band(url)

308	def institutional_authority_for_url(url: str) -> float | None:
309	    """The calibrated institutional-authority WEIGHT for ``url`` when its host is a recognized
310	    institution (enumerated registry OR the ``*.gov`` / ``*.edu`` suffix rule), else None (not an
311	    institution => no raise). None whenever the kill-switch is OFF.
312	
313	    A band_key that resolves to no band value (e.g. an override host pointed at an unknown key)
314	    is treated as "no raise" (None) rather than a wrong/zero weight. The value may also be given
315	    as a raw float directly in the registry override, in which case it is parsed here."""
316	    if not institutional_authority_enabled():
317	        return None
318	    band_key = _resolve_band(url)
319	    if band_key is None:
320	        return None
321	    bands = _band_values()
322	    if band_key in bands:
323	        return bands[band_key]
324	    # An override may store a raw float directly as the registry value.
325	    return _clamp01(band_key)

2	MoST Peptide Bond: Narrative Flow Optimization.
3	
4	Creates the backbone narrative structure. Detects dangling connectors,
5	cross-section repetition, weak transitions, and back-to-back connector
6	stutters. Provides both auto-fixes and Phase R instructions.
7	Zero LLM cost (pure embedding + regex computation).
8	
9	Cost: 0 LLM calls, ~1-2 seconds.
10	"""
11	
12	import logging
13	import os
14	import re
15	
16	import numpy as np
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionDraft
19	from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	# Thresholds (LAW VI)
24	_REPEAT_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_PEPTIDE_REPEAT_THRESHOLD", "0.80"))
25	_WEAK_TRANSITION_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_PEPTIDE_WEAK_TRANSITION", "0.15"))
26	
27	# Connectors that should not start a section
28	# FIX-047-K2: Expanded with contrastive connectors (T047 audit: "In contrast"
29	# opened Section 3 with nothing to contrast against).
30	_DANGLING_CONNECTORS = {
31	    "additionally", "furthermore", "moreover", "consequently",
32	    "similarly", "likewise", "nevertheless", "nonetheless",
33	    "indeed", "significantly", "notably",
34	    # Contrastive connectors (FIX-047-K2)
35	    "however", "conversely", "yet",
36	}
37	
38	# Multi-word dangling connectors checked separately
39	_DANGLING_PHRASES = [
40	    "in contrast",
41	    "on the other hand",
42	    "on the contrary",
43	    "as a result",
44	    "in addition",
45	    "in particular",
46	    "for this reason",
47	]
48	
49	# FIX-047-K2: Connector categories for back-to-back detection.
50	# Same-category consecutive connectors create awkward prose even when
51	# different words (e.g., "Moreover... Furthermore..." are both additive).
52	_CONNECTOR_CATEGORIES = {
53	    "additive": {"moreover", "furthermore", "additionally", "similarly",
54	                 "likewise", "indeed", "significantly", "notably",
55	                 "in addition"},
56	    "contrastive": {"however", "nevertheless", "nonetheless", "conversely",
57	                    "yet", "in contrast", "on the other hand",
58	                    "on the contrary"},
59	    "causal": {"therefore", "thus", "consequently", "hence",
60	               "as a result", "for this reason", "accordingly"},
61	}
62	
63	# Build reverse lookup: word -> category
64	_WORD_TO_CATEGORY: dict[str, str] = {}
65	for _cat, _words in _CONNECTOR_CATEGORIES.items():
66	    for _w in _words:
67	        _WORD_TO_CATEGORY[_w] = _cat
68	
69	# FIX-047-K2: Include both single-word and multi-word dangling connectors.
70	_ALL_DANGLING = sorted(
71	    list(_DANGLING_CONNECTORS) + _DANGLING_PHRASES,
72	    key=len, reverse=True,  # Longest first for greedy matching
73	)
74	_CONNECTOR_PATTERN = re.compile(
75	    r"^(" + "|".join(re.escape(c) for c in _ALL_DANGLING) + r"),?\s",
76	    re.IGNORECASE,
77	)

80	def analyze_peptide_flow(
81	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
82	) -> dict:
83	    """Run peptide flow analysis on all sections.
84	
85	    Detects:
86	    1. Dangling connectors at section starts
87	    2. Cross-section sentence repetition (embedding similarity)
88	    3. Weak transitions between adjacent sections
89	    4. Back-to-back connector stutters within sections
90	
91	    Args:
92	        sections: All SectionDraft objects.
93	
94	    Returns:
95	        Dict with keys: dangling_connectors, cross_section_repeats,
96	        weak_transitions, connector_stutters, stats.
97	    """
98	    if not sections:
99	        return _empty_result()
100	
101	    dangling_connectors = _detect_dangling_connectors(sections)
102	    connector_stutters = _detect_connector_stutters(sections)
103	    cross_section_repeats = _detect_cross_section_repeats(sections)
104	    weak_transitions = _detect_weak_transitions(sections)
105	
106	    stats = {
107	        "dangling_connectors": len(dangling_connectors),
108	        "cross_section_repeats": len(cross_section_repeats),
109	        "weak_transitions": len(weak_transitions),
110	        "connector_stutters": len(connector_stutters),
111	    }
112	
113	    logger.info(
114	        "[MoST-Peptide] Flow analysis: %d dangling connectors, "
115	        "%d cross-section repeats, %d weak transitions, %d connector stutters",
116	        stats["dangling_connectors"],
117	        stats["cross_section_repeats"],
118	        stats["weak_transitions"],
119	        stats["connector_stutters"],
120	    )
121	
122	    return {
123	        "dangling_connectors": dangling_connectors,
124	        "cross_section_repeats": cross_section_repeats,
125	        "weak_transitions": weak_transitions,
126	        "connector_stutters": connector_stutters,
127	        "stats": stats,

131	def apply_auto_fixes(
132	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
133	    peptide_result: dict,
134	) -> list[SectionDraft]:
135	    """Apply auto-fixes for dangling connectors and connector stutters.
136	
137	    Args:
138	        sections: Report sections.
139	        peptide_result: Output of analyze_peptide_flow().
140	
141	    Returns:
142	        Updated sections with auto-fixes applied.
143	    """
144	    updated = list(sections)
145	    fixes_applied = 0
146	
147	    # Fix dangling connectors at section starts
148	    dangling = peptide_result.get("dangling_connectors", [])
149	    if dangling:
150	        dangling_sids = {d["section_id"] for d in dangling}
151	        for i, sec in enumerate(updated):
152	            sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
153	            if sid not in dangling_sids:
154	                continue
155	            content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
156	            new_content = _CONNECTOR_PATTERN.sub("", content)
157	            if new_content != content:
158	                updated[i] = SectionDraft(
159	                    section_id=sid,
160	                    title=getattr(sec, "title", ""),
161	                    content=new_content.strip(),
162	                    claims_made=getattr(sec, "claims_made", []),
163	                    evidence_ids=getattr(sec, "evidence_ids", []),
164	                )
165	                fixes_applied += 1
166	
167	    # Fix back-to-back connector stutters
168	    stutters = peptide_result.get("connector_stutters", [])
169	    if stutters:
170	        stutter_sids = {s["section_id"] for s in stutters}
171	        for i, sec in enumerate(updated):
172	            sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
173	            if sid not in stutter_sids:
174	                continue
175	            content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
176	            new_content = _fix_connector_stutters(content)
177	            if new_content != content:
178	                updated[i] = SectionDraft(
179	                    section_id=sid,
180	                    title=getattr(sec, "title", ""),
181	                    content=new_content,
182	                    claims_made=getattr(sec, "claims_made", []),
183	                    evidence_ids=getattr(sec, "evidence_ids", []),
184	                )
185	                fixes_applied += 1
186	
187	    if fixes_applied > 0:
188	        logger.info("[MoST-Peptide] Applied %d auto-fixes", fixes_applied)
189	    return updated

238	def _detect_dangling_connectors(sections: list[SectionDraft]) -> list[dict]:
239	    """Detect connectors at the start of sections."""
240	    results = []
241	    for sec in sections:
242	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "").strip()
243	        if not content:
244	            continue
245	        first_sentence = content.split(".")[0] if "." in content else content[:200]
246	        match = _CONNECTOR_PATTERN.match(first_sentence)
247	        if match:
248	            results.append({
249	                "section_id": getattr(sec, "section_id", ""),
250	                "first_sentence": first_sentence[:100],
251	                "connector_word": match.group(1),
252	            })
253	    return results
254	
255	
256	def _detect_connector_stutters(sections: list[SectionDraft]) -> list[dict]:
257	    """Detect back-to-back connectors of the SAME CATEGORY within a section.
258	
259	    FIX-047-K2: T047 audit found 10 B2B connector pairs like
260	    "Moreover→Furthermore" (both additive) that the old word_a==word_b
261	    check missed. Now uses category-based matching: two consecutive
262	    connectors from the same category (additive, contrastive, causal)
263	    within 200 chars are flagged.
264	    """
265	    results = []
266	    # Build regex for ALL known connectors (single + multi-word)
267	    all_connectors = sorted(
268	        list(_DANGLING_CONNECTORS) + _DANGLING_PHRASES
269	        + ["therefore", "thus", "hence", "accordingly",
270	           "in addition", "as a result", "for this reason"],
271	        key=len, reverse=True,
272	    )
273	    connector_re = re.compile(
274	        r"\b(" + "|".join(re.escape(c) for c in all_connectors) + r")\b",
275	        re.IGNORECASE,
276	    )
277	    for sec in sections:
278	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
279	        # Find all connector occurrences
280	        matches = list(connector_re.finditer(content))
281	        if len(matches) < 2:
282	            continue
283	
284	        section_stutters = 0
285	        for i in range(len(matches) - 1):
286	            word_a = matches[i].group(1).lower()
287	            word_b = matches[i + 1].group(1).lower()
288	            gap = matches[i + 1].start() - matches[i].end()
289	            if gap >= 200:
290	                continue
291	
292	            # FIX-047-K2: Category-based matching — flag same-category pairs
293	            cat_a = _WORD_TO_CATEGORY.get(word_a, "")
294	            cat_b = _WORD_TO_CATEGORY.get(word_b, "")
295	            if cat_a and cat_b and cat_a == cat_b:
296	                results.append({
297	                    "section_id": getattr(sec, "section_id", ""),
298	                    "connector_word": f"{word_a} -> {word_b}",
299	                    "connector_category": cat_a,
300	                    "gap_chars": gap,
301	                })
302	                section_stutters += 1
303	                if section_stutters >= 3:
304	                    break  # Cap at 3 stutters per section
305	    return results
306	
307	
308	def _detect_cross_section_repeats(sections: list[SectionDraft]) -> list[dict]:
309	    """Detect sentences repeated across sections using embedding similarity."""
310	    if len(sections) < 2:
311	        return []
312	
313	    # Extract sentences per section (data-bearing only)
314	    section_sentences: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []  # (section_id, sentence, text)
315	    for sec in sections:
316	        sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
317	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
318	        # Simple sentence split
319	        sentences = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", content)
320	        for sent in sentences:
321	            sent = sent.strip()
322	            if len(sent) >= 40:  # Only meaningful sentences
323	                section_sentences.append((sid, sent[:200], sent))
324	
325	    if len(section_sentences) < 2:
326	        return []
327	
328	    # Limit to avoid embedding too many sentences
329	    max_sentences = int(os.getenv("PG_PEPTIDE_MAX_SENTENCES", "300"))
330	    if len(section_sentences) > max_sentences:
331	        section_sentences = section_sentences[:max_sentences]
332	
333	    try:
334	        texts = [s[1] for s in section_sentences]
335	        vecs = np.array(embed_texts(texts))
336	        sim = vecs @ vecs.T
337	    except Exception as exc:
338	        logger.debug(
339	            "[MoST-Peptide] Embedding failed for repeat detection: %s",
340	            str(exc)[:100],
341	        )
342	        return []
343	
344	    repeats: list[dict] = []
345	    seen_pairs: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
346	
347	    for i in range(len(section_sentences)):
348	        for j in range(i + 1, len(section_sentences)):
349	            sid_i = section_sentences[i][0]
350	            sid_j = section_sentences[j][0]
351	            if sid_i == sid_j:
352	                continue  # Same section, skip
353	            if (i, j) in seen_pairs:
354	                continue
355	
356	            if float(sim[i][j]) >= _REPEAT_THRESHOLD:
357	                seen_pairs.add((i, j))
358	                # Recommend home = section with longer content
359	                sec_i_len = len(next(
360	                    (getattr(s, "content", "") for s in sections
361	                     if getattr(s, "section_id", "") == sid_i),
362	                    "",
363	                ))
364	                sec_j_len = len(next(
365	                    (getattr(s, "content", "") for s in sections
366	                     if getattr(s, "section_id", "") == sid_j),
367	                    "",
368	                ))
369	                recommended_home = sid_i if sec_i_len >= sec_j_len else sid_j
370	
371	                repeats.append({
372	                    "sentence": section_sentences[i][1][:100],
373	                    "sections": [sid_i, sid_j],
374	                    "similarity": round(float(sim[i][j]), 3),
375	                    "recommended_home": recommended_home,
376	                })
377	
378	    # Cap results
379	    return repeats[:50]
380	
381	
382	def _detect_weak_transitions(sections: list[SectionDraft]) -> list[dict]:
383	    """Check transition strength between adjacent sections."""
384	    if len(sections) < 2:
385	        return []
386	
387	    # Get last paragraph of each section and first paragraph of the next
388	    transitions = []
389	    for i in range(len(sections) - 1):
390	        sec_a = sections[i]
391	        sec_b = sections[i + 1]
392	        content_a = getattr(sec_a, "content", "")
393	        content_b = getattr(sec_b, "content", "")
394	
395	        # Last ~200 chars of section A, first ~200 chars of section B
396	        tail_a = content_a[-200:] if len(content_a) > 200 else content_a
397	        head_b = content_b[:200] if len(content_b) > 200 else content_b
398	
399	        if tail_a and head_b:
400	            transitions.append((
401	                getattr(sec_a, "section_id", ""),
402	                getattr(sec_b, "section_id", ""),
403	                tail_a,
404	                head_b,
405	            ))
406	
407	    if not transitions:
408	        return []
409	
410	    # Embed tail/head pairs
411	    all_texts = []
412	    for _, _, tail, head in transitions:
413	        all_texts.extend([tail, head])
414	
415	    try:
416	        vecs = np.array(embed_texts(all_texts))
417	    except Exception:
418	        return []
419	
420	    weak_transitions = []
421	    for i, (sid_a, sid_b, _, _) in enumerate(transitions):
422	        tail_vec = vecs[i * 2]
423	        head_vec = vecs[i * 2 + 1]
424	        score = float(np.dot(tail_vec, head_vec))
425	        if score < _WEAK_TRANSITION_THRESHOLD:
426	            weak_transitions.append({
427	                "from_section": sid_a,
428	                "to_section": sid_b,
429	                "score": round(score, 3),
430	            })
431	
432	    return weak_transitions
433	
434	
435	def _fix_connector_stutters(content: str) -> str:
436	    """Remove duplicate connectors within close proximity.
437	
438	    FIX-059-H: Uses category-based matching consistent with detection
439	    at _detect_connector_stutters(). Old code used word_a == word_b
440	    (exact match only), causing 29 category-based stutters to be
441	    detected but 0 fixed.
442	    """
443	    # FIX-059-H: Use ALL connectors (single + multi-word) for matching,
444	    # consistent with detection regex at _detect_connector_stutters()
445	    all_connectors = sorted(
446	        list(_DANGLING_CONNECTORS) + _DANGLING_PHRASES
447	        + ["therefore", "thus", "hence", "accordingly",
448	           "in addition", "as a result", "for this reason"],
449	        key=len, reverse=True,
450	    )
451	    connector_re = re.compile(
452	        r"\b(" + "|".join(re.escape(c) for c in all_connectors) + r")\b",
453	        re.IGNORECASE,
454	    )
455	    matches = list(connector_re.finditer(content))
456	    if len(matches) < 2:
457	        return content
458	
459	    # FIX-059-H: Find back-to-back same-CATEGORY connectors, remove the second
460	    removals = []
461	    for i in range(len(matches) - 1):
462	        word_a = matches[i].group(1).lower()
463	        word_b = matches[i + 1].group(1).lower()
464	        gap = matches[i + 1].start() - matches[i].end()
465	        # FIX-059-H: Category-based matching (was: word_a == word_b)
466	        cat_a = _WORD_TO_CATEGORY.get(word_a, word_a)
467	        cat_b = _WORD_TO_CATEGORY.get(word_b, word_b)
468	        if cat_a == cat_b and gap < 200:
469	            # Remove the second connector + trailing comma/space
470	            start = matches[i + 1].start()
471	            end = matches[i + 1].end()
472	            # Also remove trailing comma and space
473	            while end < len(content) and content[end] in ", ":
474	                end += 1
475	            removals.append((start, end))
476	
477	    if not removals:
478	        return content
479	
480	    # Apply removals from end to start to preserve indices
481	    result = content
482	    for start, end in reversed(removals):
483	        result = result[:start] + result[end:]
484	    return result
485	
486	
487	def _empty_result() -> dict:
488	    """Return empty peptide result."""
489	    return {
490	        "dangling_connectors": [],
491	        "cross_section_repeats": [],
492	        "weak_transitions": [],
493	        "connector_stutters": [],
494	        "stats": {
495	            "dangling_connectors": 0,
496	            "cross_section_repeats": 0,
497	            "weak_transitions": 0,
498	            "connector_stutters": 0,
499	        },
500	    }

66	        (final_markdown, assembly_stats)
67	    """
68	    # Fix R6-#5: Prune placeholder and failed sections
69	    active_sections, pruned_count = _prune_sections(sections, section_order)
70	
71	    if not active_sections:
72	        logger.error("Assembly: ALL sections pruned — no content to assemble")
73	        return _empty_report(title, query), {"error": "all_sections_pruned"}
74	
75	    # Build body markdown
76	    body_parts: list[str] = [f"# {title}\n"]
77	
78	    for section_id in section_order:
79	        if section_id not in active_sections:
80	            continue
81	        sec = active_sections[section_id]
82	        # Normalize citations in each section
83	        content = normalize_citations(sec["content"])
84	        body_parts.append(f"\n## {sec['title']}\n\n{content}\n")
85	
86	    body = "\n".join(body_parts)
87	
88	    # Fix R6-#2: Grounded bibliography (2-pass process)
89	    #
90	    # Pass 1: Extract [SRC-NNN] tags the LLM actually printed in the text.
91	    #          This is the ONLY source of truth for the bibliography.
92	    #
93	    # Pass 2: Build a unique, ordered list of these tags, map to [1], [2],
94	    #          and generate the bibliography strictly from extracted tags.
95	    cited_src_ids = _extract_cited_sources(body)
96	
97	    if not cited_src_ids:
98	        logger.warning("Assembly: 0 citations found in text — bibliography empty")
99	
100	    # FIX-V2-STATS: Capture citation stats BEFORE resolve_to_numbers()
101	    # converts [SRC-NNN] → [N]. citation_stats() searches for [SRC-NNN],
102	    # so it must run on the pre-resolution text.
103	    pre_resolution_cite_stats = citation_stats(body)
104	
105	    # Build citation map: SRC-NNN -> sequential number
106	    citation_map: dict[str, int] = {}
107	    bibliography_lines: list[str] = []
108	
109	    for i, src_id in enumerate(cited_src_ids, 1):
110	        citation_map[src_id] = i
111	        entry = registry.get(src_id)
112	        if entry:
113	            bib_line = _format_bibliography_entry(i, entry)
114	            bibliography_lines.append(bib_line)
115	        else:
116	            logger.warning("Assembly: %s cited in text but not in registry", src_id)
117	            bibliography_lines.append(f"[{i}] {src_id} — source not found in registry")
118	
119	    # Replace [SRC-NNN] with [N] in the body
120	    body = resolve_to_numbers(body, citation_map)
121	
122	    # Append bibliography
123	    if bibliography_lines:
124	        body += "\n\n## References\n\n"
125	        body += "\n".join(bibliography_lines)
126	        body += "\n"
127	
128	    # Compute stats (using pre-resolution citation counts)
129	    stats = _compute_stats(
130	        body, sections, active_sections, cited_src_ids,
131	        pruned_count, pre_resolution_cite_stats,
132	    )
133	    logger.info(
134	        "Assembly complete: %d sections, %d citations, %d unique sources, %d words, %d pruned",
135	        stats["active_sections"], stats["total_citations"],
136	        stats["unique_sources"], stats["total_words"], stats["pruned_sections"],
137	    )
138	
139	    return body, stats
140	
141	
142	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
143	# Fix R6-#5: Outline pruning
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	
146	def _prune_sections(
147	    sections: dict[str, ReportSection],
148	    section_order: list[str],
149	) -> tuple[dict[str, ReportSection], int]:
150	    """Remove placeholder and failed sections from the output.
151	
152	    Fix R6-#5: If search performs poorly and 5/15 sections get the
153	    placeholder, the final report would be littered with ugly empty
154	    sections. Gemini just skips them — so do we.
155	
156	    Returns:
157	        (active_sections, pruned_count)
158	    """
159	    active: dict[str, ReportSection] = {}
160	    pruned = 0
161	
162	    for section_id in section_order:
163	        if section_id not in sections:
164	            pruned += 1
165	            continue
166	
167	        sec = sections[section_id]
168	        content = sec.get("content", "")
169	
170	        # Check for placeholder markers
171	        is_placeholder = any(marker in content for marker in _PLACEHOLDER_MARKERS)
172	
173	        # Also skip sections with zero meaningful content
174	        is_empty = len(content.strip()) < 50
175	
176	        if is_placeholder or is_empty:
177	            pruned += 1
178	            logger.info(
179	                "Assembly: pruned section '%s' (%s)",
180	                sec.get("title", section_id),
181	                "placeholder" if is_placeholder else "empty",
182	            )
183	            continue
184	
185	        active[section_id] = sec
186	
187	    if pruned > 0:
188	        logger.info(
189	            "Assembly: pruned %d/%d sections (placeholders/empty)",
190	            pruned, pruned + len(active),
191	        )
192	
193	    return active, pruned
194	
195	
196	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	# Fix R6-#2: Grounded bibliography extraction
198	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
199	
200	def _extract_cited_sources(text: str) -> list[str]:
201	    """Extract unique [SRC-NNN] tags from text in order of first appearance.
202	
203	    Fix R6-#2: This is the ONLY input to bibliography generation.
204	    Sources assigned by the Blueprint but not actually cited by the LLM
205	    are intentionally excluded.
206	
207	    Returns:
208	        Ordered list of unique SRC-NNN IDs (e.g., ["SRC-001", "SRC-003", "SRC-007"]).
209	    """
210	    # Find all SRC-NNN references in order of appearance
211	    all_refs = re.findall(r"\[SRC-(\d{3})\]", text)
212	
213	    # Deduplicate while preserving first-appearance order
214	    seen: set[str] = set()
215	    unique: list[str] = []
216	    for ref_num in all_refs:
217	        src_id = f"SRC-{ref_num}"
218	        if src_id not in seen:
219	            seen.add(src_id)
220	            unique.append(src_id)
221	
222	    return unique
223	
224	
225	def _format_bibliography_entry(number: int, entry: Any) -> str:
226	    """Format a single bibliography entry.
227	
228	    Args:
229	        number: Citation number (1-indexed).
230	        entry: SourceEntry from SourceRegistry.
231	    """
232	    parts = [f"[{number}]"]
233	
234	    # Authors
235	    if entry.authors:
236	        if len(entry.authors) <= 3:
237	            parts.append(", ".join(entry.authors))
238	        else:
239	            parts.append(f"{entry.authors[0]} et al.")
240	
241	    # Year
242	    if entry.year:
243	        parts.append(f"({entry.year}).")
244	
245	    # Title
246	    parts.append(f'"{entry.title}."')
247	
248	    # Venue
249	    if entry.venue:
250	        parts.append(f"*{entry.venue}*.")
251	
252	    # DOI or URL
253	    if entry.doi:
254	        parts.append(f"DOI: {entry.doi}")
255	    elif entry.url:
256	        parts.append(entry.url)
257	
258	    return " ".join(parts)
259	
260	
261	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
262	# Stats and empty report
263	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
264	
265	def _compute_stats(
266	    final_text: str,
267	    all_sections: dict[str, ReportSection],
268	    active_sections: dict[str, ReportSection],
269	    cited_src_ids: list[str],
270	    pruned_count: int,
271	    pre_resolution_cite_stats: dict[str, int] | None = None,
272	) -> dict[str, Any]:
273	    """Compute assembly statistics for quality tracking.
274	
275	    FIX-V2-STATS: Uses pre-resolution citation stats (counted before
276	    [SRC-NNN] → [N] conversion) for accurate citation metrics.
277	    """
278	    words = len(final_text.split())
279	
280	    # Use pre-resolution stats if available (accurate), else fall back
281	    cite_stats = pre_resolution_cite_stats or citation_stats(final_text)
282	
283	    return {
284	        "total_sections": len(all_sections),
285	        "active_sections": len(active_sections),
286	        "pruned_sections": pruned_count,
287	        "total_words": words,
288	        "total_citations": cite_stats["total_citations"],
289	        "unique_sources": len(cited_src_ids),
290	        "uncited_paragraphs": cite_stats["uncited_paragraphs"],
291	        "total_paragraphs": cite_stats["total_paragraphs"],
292	        "compound_violations": cite_stats["compound_violations"],
293	        "citations_per_100_words": round(
294	            cite_stats["total_citations"] / max(words, 1) * 100, 2
295	        ),
296	    }
297	
298	
299	def _empty_report(title: str, query: str) -> str:
300	    """Generate a minimal report when all sections are pruned."""
301	    return (
302	        f"# {title}\n\n"
303	        f"> [!WARNING]\n"
304	        f"> Insufficient evidence was retrieved to generate this report.\n"
305	        f"> Query: {query}\n"
306	        f">\n"
307	        f"> Please try refining the research query or expanding search parameters.\n"
308	    )
309

13	def sync_evidence_ids_from_content(section: SectionDraft) -> SectionDraft:
14	    """Rebuild evidence_ids from actual [CITE:ev_xxx] markers in content.
15	
16	    M-01: Fixes 3x underreporting where evidence_ids field (40 unique, 16.5%)
17	    diverges from actual CITE markers in content (121 unique, 49.8%).
18	
19	    Args:
20	        section: A SectionDraft whose evidence_ids may be stale.
21	
22	    Returns:
23	        A new SectionDraft with evidence_ids matching actual content citations.
24	        If no CITE markers found, returns the section unchanged.
25	    """
26	    content = getattr(section, "content", "")
27	    actual_cites = list(set(re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]", content)))
28	    if not actual_cites:
29	        return section
30	    return SectionDraft(
31	        section_id=getattr(section, "section_id", ""),
32	        title=getattr(section, "title", ""),
33	        content=content,
34	        claims_made=getattr(section, "claims_made", []),
35	        evidence_ids=actual_cites,
36	    )

1	"""
2	Report assembler for polaris graph.
3	
4	Merges sections, resolves citations to numbered references,
5	adds transitions, builds bibliography. Pure code — no LLM calls.
6	"""
7	
8	import logging
9	import os
10	import random
11	import re
12	
13	import pysbd
14	
15	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import CitationAudit, ReportOutline, SectionDraft
16	from src.polaris_graph.state import BibliographyEntry, EvidencePiece, ReportSection
17	from src.polaris_graph.tracing import get_tracer
18	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.citation_mapper import (
19	    build_bibliography,
20	    resolve_citations,
21	    strip_ungrounded_citations,
22	    CITE_PATTERN,
23	)
24	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_writer import _clean_artifacts
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	# FIX-047C: PySBD segmenter handles U.S., Dr., Prof., e.g., i.e., et al. correctly
29	# (97.92% accuracy vs 92.9% spaCy, 92.2% NLTK)
30	_segmenter = pysbd.Segmenter(language="en", clean=False)
31	
32	
33	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34	# W3 (I-deepfix-001) — surface the ALREADY-COMPUTED per-citation credibility WEIGHT
35	# in the render, and reconcile the legacy GOLD/SILVER quality-tier vocabulary onto
36	# the T1-T7 credibility scheme so ONE credibility language shows.
37	#
38	# DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): pure render-surfacing of state that credibility_pass /
39	# tier_classifier / document_type_classifier ALREADY computed (tier T1-T7,
40	# authority_score, source_class, document_type genre). NO source is dropped, NO
41	# gate reads these — advisory disclosure only, the SAME class as the existing
42	# credibility_weight. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
43	# provenance / span-grounding) is untouched.
44	#
45	# Default-OFF byte-identical revert (LAW VI): PG_RENDER_CITATION_CREDIBILITY gates
46	# the per-reference annotation; an evidence row with NO credibility signal yields
47	# an empty annotation (fail-open — a weight is never fabricated).
48	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49	
50	_RENDER_CREDIBILITY_FLAG = "PG_RENDER_CITATION_CREDIBILITY"
51	
52	# Legacy GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE -> T1-T7 band reconciliation. The legacy 3-tier
53	# vocabulary predates the T1-T7 authority scheme; when ONLY the legacy tier is
54	# present we map it to the closest T1-T7 band so the render + metrics speak ONE
55	# credibility language. GOLD = peer-reviewed / regulatory (T1-T3 band ->
56	# representative T2); SILVER = review / secondary (T4); BRONZE = news / blog (T6).
57	_LEGACY_TIER_TO_T = {
58	    "GOLD": "T2",
59	    "SILVER": "T4",
60	    "BRONZE": "T6",
61	}
62	_T_TIER_RE = re.compile(r"^T[1-7]$")
63	
64	
65	def _render_citation_credibility_active() -> bool:
66	    """The PG_RENDER_CITATION_CREDIBILITY leg (default OFF => byte-identical)."""
67	    return os.getenv(_RENDER_CREDIBILITY_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() in (
68	        "1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled",
69	    )
70	
71	
72	def resolve_credibility_tier(evidence: dict) -> "tuple[str | None, str]":
73	    """Resolve a source's T1-T7 credibility tier from an evidence row.
74	
75	    Prefers the tier_classifier output (``source_tier`` / ``tier`` when it is a
76	    real ``T1``..``T7`` label). Falls back to reconciling the legacy
77	    GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE ``quality_tier`` onto the closest T1-T7 band so a report
78	    that only carries legacy tiers still speaks ONE credibility language. Returns
79	    ``(tier_label, basis)``; ``(None, "unresolved")`` when nothing credible is
80	    present — never fabricated (§-1.3 / LAW II fail-loud-not-guess).
81	    """
82	    for key in ("source_tier", "tier"):
83	        raw = str(evidence.get(key) or "").strip().upper()
84	        if _T_TIER_RE.match(raw):
85	            return raw, f"tier_classifier:{key}"
86	    for key in ("quality_tier", "tier"):
87	        raw = str(evidence.get(key) or "").strip().upper()
88	        if raw in _LEGACY_TIER_TO_T:
89	            return _LEGACY_TIER_TO_T[raw], f"legacy_reconcile:{raw}"
90	    return None, "unresolved"
91	
92	
93	def format_citation_credibility_annotation(evidence: dict) -> str:
94	    """A one-line per-citation credibility+genre disclosure appended to a
95	    reference in the rendered ``## References`` block.
96	
97	    Surfaces the ALREADY-COMPUTED per-source signals — tier (T1-T7),
98	    authority_score, source_class, and document_type genre. It is per-SOURCE
99	    (not per-claim), so it deliberately does NOT assert basket corroboration
100	    (that is a claim-level signal surfaced by disclosure_population). Returns ""
101	    when no credibility signal is present (fail-open — a weight is never
102	    invented, so an unclassified source renders exactly as before).
103	    """
104	    bits: list[str] = []
105	    tier, _ = resolve_credibility_tier(evidence)
106	    if tier:
107	        bits.append(f"tier {tier}")
108	    authority = evidence.get("authority_score")
109	    if isinstance(authority, (int, float)) and not isinstance(authority, bool):
110	        bits.append(f"authority {float(authority):.2f}")
111	    source_class = str(evidence.get("source_class") or "").strip()
112	    if source_class:
113	        bits.append(f"class {source_class}")
114	    document_type = str(evidence.get("document_type") or "").strip()
115	    if document_type:
116	        bits.append(f"genre {document_type}")
117	    if not bits:
118	        return ""
119	    return "[credibility: " + "; ".join(bits) + "]"
120	
121	
122	def _reference_lines(
123	    bibliography: list, ev_by_id: dict, render_cred: bool,
124	) -> list[str]:
125	    """Formatted ``## References`` lines, optionally annotated with the W3
126	    per-citation credibility WEIGHT. ``assemble_report`` rebuilds the References
127	    block at several post-processing stages; this single helper is used at EVERY
128	    such site so the annotation renders regardless of which rebuild produces the
129	    final ``report.md``. The annotation is derived FRESH from the evidence row
130	    each time (never stored in ``formatted``), so citation renumber/cleanup can
131	    never orphan or double it. ``render_cred=False`` => the legacy bare line."""
132	    lines: list[str] = []
133	    for entry in bibliography:
134	        line = entry["formatted"]
135	        if render_cred:
136	            _rep_ev: dict = {}
137	            for _eid in entry.get("evidence_ids", []):
138	                if _eid in ev_by_id:
139	                    _rep_ev = ev_by_id[_eid]
140	                    break
141	            _annot = format_citation_credibility_annotation(_rep_ev)
142	            if _annot:
143	                line = f"{line} {_annot}"
144	        lines.append(line)
145	    return lines
146	
147	
148	def _split_sentences(text: str, min_len: int = 0) -> list[str]:
149	    """FIX-047C: Split text into sentences using PySBD.
150	
151	    Correctly handles abbreviations (U.S., Dr., Prof., Jan., e.g., i.e., et al.)
152	    that regex-based splitting gets wrong, causing garbled output like
153	    "U.S. In addition, environmental Protection Agency".
154	
155	    Args:
156	        text: Text to split into sentences.
157	        min_len: Minimum character length for returned sentences (0 = no filter).
158	
159	    Returns:
160	        List of sentence strings, optionally filtered by min_len.
161	    """
162	    if not text or not text.strip():
163	        return []
164	    sentences = _segmenter.segment(text)
165	    if min_len > 0:
166	        return [s.strip() for s in sentences if len(s.strip()) >= min_len]
167	    return [s.strip() for s in sentences if s.strip()]
168	
169	
170	def _validate_abstract_metrics(
171	    abstract: str,
172	    unique_sources: int,
173	    total_citations: int,
174	    total_words: int,
175	) -> list[str]:
176	    """FIX-E2: Validate numeric claims in abstract against computed metrics."""
177	    warnings: list[str] = []
178	
179	    # Find all numbers in the abstract
180	    numbers_in_abstract = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', abstract)
181	
182	    for num_str in numbers_in_abstract:
183	        num = int(num_str)
184	        # Check if this number could be a source count claim
185	        if 10 <= num <= 500 and abs(num - unique_sources) > 5 and num != total_citations:
186	            # Might be a hallucinated source count
187	            try:
188	                idx = abstract.index(num_str)
189	            except ValueError:
190	                continue
191	            context_start = max(0, idx - 30)
192	            context_end = idx + 30
193	            context_window = abstract[context_start:context_end].lower()
194	            source_keywords = [
195	                "source", "study", "studies", "paper",
196	                "article", "reference",
197	            ]
198	            if any(word in context_window for word in source_keywords):
199	                warnings.append(
200	                    f"Abstract claims '{num_str}' near source-related text "
201	                    f"but actual unique sources = {unique_sources}"
202	                )
203	
204	    return warnings
205	
206	
207	def _remove_orphan_citations(
208	    text: str,
209	    valid_numbers: set[int],
210	) -> tuple[str, int]:
211	    """FIX-045A: Remove citation numbers that have no bibliography entry.
212	
213	    After quality gate expansion, the LLM may invent [N] citation numbers
214	    beyond the bibliography range. This removes them to prevent dangling refs.
215	
216	    Returns (cleaned_text, count_removed).
217	    """
218	    if not valid_numbers:
219	        return text, 0
220	
221	    orphan_count = 0
222	
223	    def _check(match: re.Match) -> str:
224	        nonlocal orphan_count
225	        num = int(match.group(1))
226	        if num not in valid_numbers:
227	            orphan_count += 1
228	            return ""
229	        return match.group(0)
230	
231	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", _check, text)
232	    cleaned = re.sub(r"  +", " ", cleaned)
233	    return cleaned, orphan_count
234	
235	
236	def _clean_citation_artifacts(text: str) -> str:
237	    """FIX-047A + M-04: Clean citation artifacts.
238	
239	    Handles:
240	    - ' .' (space before punctuation from citation removal)
241	    - '..' (double periods from citation removal at sentence boundaries)
242	    - Multiple consecutive spaces
243	    - M-04: Orphaned citations between connectors ("Moreover, [16] Consequently,")
244	    """
245	    # M-04: Fix orphaned citations between connectors: "Moreover, [16] Consequently," -> "Consequently,"
246	    connectors = r"(?:Moreover|Furthermore|Additionally|Indeed|Significantly|Consequently|Notably)"
247	    text = re.sub(
248	        rf"({connectors}),?\s*(\[\d+\](?:\s*\[\d+\])*)\s*({connectors})",
249	        r"\3",
250	        text,
251	    )
252	    # Remove space before punctuation: " ." -> ".", " ," -> ","
253	    text = re.sub(r'\s+([.,;:!?])', r'\1', text)
254	    # Clean double/triple periods: ".." or ". ." -> "."
255	    text = re.sub(r'\.\s*\.', '.', text)
256	    # Clean leftover multiple spaces
257	    text = re.sub(r'  +', ' ', text)
258	    return text
259	
260	
261	def _fix_number_spacing(text: str) -> str:
262	    """FIX-045E: Fix spacing errors in numbers like '99. 9%' -> '99.9%'."""
263	    # Fix decimal-space-digit-percent: "99. 9%" -> "99.9%"
264	    text = re.sub(r"(\d+)\.\s+(\d+)(%)", r"\1.\2\3", text)
265	    # Fix decimal-space-digit in general: "3. 14" -> "3.14"
266	    text = re.sub(r"(\d+)\.\s+(\d+)(?=\s|[,;.]|$)", r"\1.\2", text)
267	    return text
268	
269	
270	def _fix_abstract_metrics(
271	    abstract: str,
272	    unique_sources: int,
273	    total_citations: int,
274	    total_words: int,
275	) -> str:
276	    """FIX-045C: Recompute abstract metrics from actual content.
277	
278	    Finds patterns like '10,418 words', '23 sources', '218 citations'
279	    in the abstract and replaces with actual computed values.
280	    """
281	    fixed = abstract
282	
283	    # Fix word count: "N words" or "N,NNN words"
284	    def _fix_words(match: re.Match) -> str:
285	        return f"{total_words:,} words"
286	    fixed = re.sub(r"[\d,]+\s+words?\b", _fix_words, fixed)
287	
288	    # Fix source count: "N sources" / "N studies" / "N papers" / "N references"
289	    def _fix_sources(match: re.Match) -> str:
290	        return f"{unique_sources} {match.group(1)}"
291	    fixed = re.sub(
292	        r"\d+\s+(sources?|studies|papers?|references?)\b",
293	        _fix_sources, fixed,
294	    )
295	
296	    # FIX-D5: Fix citation count — broadened to catch "N total citations",
297	    # "N in-text citations", "N referenced citations" etc.
298	    def _fix_citations(match: re.Match) -> str:
299	        return f"{total_citations} {match.group(1)}"
300	    fixed = re.sub(
301	        r"\d+\s+(?:total\s+|in-text\s+|referenced\s+)?(citations?)\b",
302	        _fix_citations, fixed,
303	    )
304	
305	    # FIX-R9: Remove self-referential word count from abstract
306	    # "The 10,983-word report" is fragile — wrong if report is edited later
307	    fixed = re.sub(r'(?i)the\s+\d[\d,]*-word\s+report', 'This report', fixed)
308	    fixed = re.sub(r'(?i)this\s+\d[\d,]*-word\s+report', 'This report', fixed)
309	
310	    if fixed != abstract:
311	        logger.info(
312	            "[polaris graph] FIX-045C: Abstract metrics recomputed: "
313	            "%d words, %d citations, %d sources",
314	            total_words, total_citations, unique_sources,
315	        )
316	
317	    return fixed
